reason: tests need rewrite, not instruction. The failing rubrics solution_uniqueness_guard, tests_verify_behavior_not_text, and test_not_tautological are test design issues - the fail-to-pass tests (test_hub_pages_structure, test_stale_pages_link_to_hubs, test_test_uses_hub_navigation, test_agents_md_router_act_rule, test_router_act_skill_created) are string-in-file content checks that assert on exact substrings like 'export default function Page()', 'await connection()', 'LinkAccordion', hub-a/hub-b/hub-c paths, 'name: router-act' frontmatter, 'no-requests', 'BFCache'. The no_solution_leakage and instruction_no_hint_leakage failures are caused by the instruction needing to specify exact content patterns to enable the agent to pass these text-matching tests. This task cannot be reconciled through instruction.md changes alone.
